reporting_templates.name: Weekly Performance Report — Media Team

sections.title: Week Summary
sections.description: One-paragraph narrative of the week's overall performance vs. prior week and vs. targets.

sections.title: Campaign Scorecards
sections.description: One row per active campaign: actual vs. target for primary KPIs, pacing status (on/over/under), and a 1-line note.

sections.title: Budget Pacing
sections.description: Total spend vs. budget for each campaign. Flag anything >10% over or under expected pacing.

sections.title: Top/Bottom Creative
sections.description: Top 3 and bottom 3 performing creatives by primary KPI across all campaigns.

sections.title: Action Items
sections.description: Bulleted list of specific actions to take next week: budget shifts, bid changes, creative swaps, pauses.

reporting_templates.notes: Sent every Monday morning for prior week (Mon-Sun). Owner: each team lead. Distributed to team + manager.

reporting_templates.name: Monthly Executive Report

sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.description: 3-5 bullet points: total spend, revenue, ROAS, key wins, key challenges. No jargon.

sections.title: Performance vs. Targets
sections.description: Table comparing actual vs. target for each team's primary KPIs. RAG status (red/amber/green).

sections.title: Channel Mix & Budget Allocation
sections.description: How budget was split across channels and whether it aligns with strategy. Include recommended adjustments.

sections.title: Brand Health Indicators
sections.description: Brand team results: reach, frequency, brand lift (if study ran), share of voice estimate.

sections.title: Month-over-Month Trends
sections.description: Key metric trends vs. prior month and vs. same month last year where available.

sections.title: Next Month Plan
sections.description: Planned budget, key campaigns launching, tests running, and major optimizations.

reporting_templates.notes: Delivered first business day of the following month. Audience: CEO, CMO, VP Marketing, Finance. Keep to 1 page slides + appendix.

reporting_templates.name: Daily Pacing Dashboard

sections.title: Pacing Status Overview
sections.description: Table of all active campaigns: status (on/over/under), spend to date, expected spend, variance %.

sections.title: Campaigns Requiring Action
sections.description: Campaigns >10% over or under pacing. Recommended actions for each.

reporting_templates.notes: Auto-updated daily via Looker Studio connected to data warehouse. Alert sent via Slack #paid-media-alerts when any campaign is >15% off pace.

sections.title: Model Comparison Summary
sections.description: Side-by-side conversion counts and channel credit under each attribution model.

sections.title: Top Conversion Paths
sections.description: Most common channel sequences that lead to conversion. Highlights multi-touch journey patterns.

sections.title: Attribution Delta by Channel
sections.description: How each channel's credited conversions change between last-click and data-driven/linear. Identifies under/over-credited channels.

sections.title: Recommendation
sections.description: Based on path data and deltas, recommend which attribution model should drive bid optimization for each campaign type.

reporting_templates.name: Channel Mix & QBR Report

sections.title: Strategic Overview
sections.description: How current channel mix aligns with company objectives (brand vs. performance split, funnel coverage).

sections.title: Channel Performance Ranking
sections.description: Channels ranked by efficiency (ROAS or CPA), reach, and strategic fit.

sections.title: Quarter-over-Quarter Shifts
sections.description: How spend allocation changed vs. prior quarter and the performance impact.

sections.title: Next Quarter Allocation Recommendation
sections.description: Proposed budget split for next quarter with rationale. Includes test-and-learn budget line.

reporting_templates.notes: Presented at QBR — prepare 10-minute presentation version. Audience includes CFO. All $ figures rounded to nearest $1k.
